title: So whats up with Unit 00s paint job
display_title: So whats up with Unit 00s paint job
wikitext:
[]: So what's up with Unit 00's paint job?             Quote Post by Bread » Tue Jan 08, 2013 10:37 pm So it started as yellow, and changed to blue. Who is responsible for Eva aesthetics at Nerv, why the change, who conned Keel/Gendou into buying getting that much new paint (given how much they spent repairing unit-00 in the first place.)? I guess they could have just had it lying around. Now don't get me wrong, I prefer the blue. It would just make more sense if it were at least a similar blue to that of unit-03, as the first branch in Massachusets may have sent the unit-03 paint along with the eva itself. But this is chit-chat. Silliness and creativity are encouraged in explaining motivation. Fanwank away!

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Tue Jan 08, 2013 11:11 pm The orange/gray color scheme is a common one for testing prototype vehicles the world over. It makes sense that when Unit 00 was simply being tested as a prototype that it has this color scheme, only to be changed when the unit was made operational.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Tue Jan 08, 2013 11:55 pm I think they kept the orange/grey color scheme in Rebuild because the blue/white one they used in NGE would be too similar to the Mark.06.

[]:             Quote Post by ONIAgent150 » Wed Jan 09, 2013 1:34 am Tribblepoo wrote: The orange/gray color scheme is a common one for testing prototype vehicles the world over. It makes sense that when Unit 00 was simply being tested as a prototype that it has this color scheme, only to be changed when the unit was made operational.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Wed Jan 09, 2013 5:53 am They were preparing unit-00 for winter fashion season, summer was over and so was orange, blue was like, soooo trendy that winter, seriously, Rei wouldn't be caught dead with an Eva out of fashion, it would be like, sooo embarassing.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jan 09, 2013 7:08 am Almost the entire armor was molten, they probably replaced it completely so they would've needed to paint it one way or another. So why not ask Rei if she wants the next paintjob to be a less garish color...?

[]:             Quote Post by Blood Type: Blue » Wed Jan 09, 2013 7:30 am I thought it was done by Anno to further highlight the red/blue theme between Asuka and Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Jan 09, 2013 7:37 am Kendrix wrote: So why not ask Rei if she wants the next paintjob to be a less garish color...?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Jan 09, 2013 1:58 pm Basically, after Operation Yashima Unit-00 got a decent overhaul: new armor, new equipment (ie, shoulder pylons), and a new paint job. Why a new paint job? Because yellow is a terrible color. That and prototype designations and blah blah blah.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jan 09, 2013 2:25 pm See also: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6414/Color-observation-question-about-the-evangelions/

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jan 09, 2013 2:57 pm Kendrix wrote: So why not ask Rei if she wants the next paintjob to be a less garish color...?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jan 09, 2013 3:37 pm Why would they not ask her, really? Its not like it's in any way important to her employers what *color* the mecha is as long as she pilots it, they don't have any reason for any particular color, it seems like the most natural thing to just ask her, say, they're having a tube session, Ritsuko's briefing Gendo on the progress of the repairs, the subject of the paint job just comes up, and... well, somehow it ends up blue. Also, sheer coinicidence that it got painted blue after she had her backside saved by someone who's apparently a fan of the color in question. The contrast with Asuka is most certainly the doylist explanation of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Jan 09, 2013 4:25 pm Blood Type: Blue wrote: I thought it was done by Anno to further highlight the red/blue theme between Asuka and Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jan 09, 2013 4:28 pm Kendrix wrote: it seems like the most natural thing to just ask her, say, they're having a tube session, Ritsuko's briefing Gendo on the progress of the repairs, the subject of the paint job just comes up, and... well, somehow it ends up blue.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Wed Jan 09, 2013 4:57 pm She just chose the colour scheme in the character select screen, gosh you guys.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jan 09, 2013 4:58 pm Get outta here; this ain't super bash sisters. I congratulate the .05% of you that may get the joke.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jan 09, 2013 6:08 pm They don't have to care particularly much about Rei to ask her what color she wants her mecha. It's a trivial little background thingy that could be conveniently added to the end of any given conversation. EVA 01 is really the odd one out, not even a primary color, and little resemblance to the pilot's uniform (Rei's suit has little orange elements, EVA 00 has bits of white) maybe it's purple because Gendo knows Yui likes puple or something? She was frequently wearing a purple shirt.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Wed Jan 09, 2013 6:15 pm Eva-01 is royalty. It's obvious, all the other evas are commoners in comparison.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Wed Jan 09, 2013 6:43 pm Kendrix wrote: They don't have to care particularly much about Rei to ask her what color she wants her mecha. It's a trivial little background thingy that could be conveniently added to the end of any given conversation. EVA 01 is really the odd one out, not even a primary color, and little resemblance to the pilot's uniform (Rei's suit has little orange elements, EVA 00 has bits of white) maybe it's purple because Gendo knows Yui likes puple or something? She was frequently wearing a purple shirt.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Wed Jan 09, 2013 8:33 pm Kendrix wrote: They don't have to care particularly much about Rei to ask her what color she wants her mecha. It's a trivial little background thingy that could be conveniently added to the end of any given conversation.
